nouns.colors.red: Red is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a fire truck and a strawberry are red.
You can say 'I see red!' or 'I want the red one.'
nouns.colors.blue: Blue is a color you can see all around you.
Things like the sky and the ocean are blue.
You can say 'I see blue!' or 'I want the blue one.'
nouns.colors.green: Green is a color you can see all around you.
Things like grass and a leaf are green.
You can say 'I see green!' or 'I want the green one.'
nouns.colors.yellow: Yellow is a color you can see all around you.
Things like the sun and a banana are yellow.
You can say 'I see yellow!' or 'I want the yellow one.'
nouns.colors.orange: Orange is a color you can see all around you.
Things like an orange and a pumpkin are orange.
You can say 'I see orange!' or 'I want the orange one.'
nouns.colors.purple: Purple is a color you can see all around you.

nouns.colors.pink: Pink is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a flower and bubblegum are pink.

nouns.colors.black: Black is a color you can see all around you.
Things like the night sky and a tire are black.
You can say 'I see black!' or 'I want the black one.'
nouns.colors.white: White is a color you can see all around you.
Things like snow and a cloud are white.
You can say 'I see white!' or 'I want the white one.'
nouns.colors.brown: Brown is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a tree trunk and chocolate are brown.
You can say 'I see brown!' or 'I want the brown one.'
needs.numbers.one: It is the very first counting number, right before two.
You can count to one or hold up one finger.

You can count to 2 or hold up 2 fingers.

needs.numbers.nine: It comes after eight and before ten.

needs.numbers.ten: It comes after nine and before eleven.
You can count to 10 or hold up 10 fingers.
needs.abc.a: A is a letter in the alphabet.
It makes the 'ah or ay' sound.
You can find A in words like 'apple' and 'ant'.
needs.abc.b: B is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.q: Q is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.r: R is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.s: S is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.t: T is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.u: U is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.v: V is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.w: W is a letter in the alphabet.

needs.abc.z: Z is a letter in the alphabet.

nouns.colors.more.gray: Gray is a color you can see all around you.
Things like an elephant and a rain cloud are gray.
You can say 'I see gray!' or 'I want the gray one.'
nouns.colors.more.gold: Gold is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a shiny crown and a coin are gold.
You can say 'I see gold!' or 'I want the gold one.'
nouns.colors.more.silver: Silver is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a spoon and a shiny key are silver.
You can say 'I see silver!' or 'I want the silver one.'
nouns.colors.more.lime: Lime is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a lime and bright grass are lime.
You can say 'I see lime!' or 'I want the lime one.'
nouns.colors.more.teal: Teal is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a peacock feather and shallow sea water are teal.
You can say 'I see teal!' or 'I want the teal one.'
nouns.colors.more.navy: Navy is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a dark blue sky and blue jeans are navy.
You can say 'I see navy!' or 'I want the navy one.'
nouns.colors.more.peach: Peach is a color you can see all around you.
Things like a peach and a sunset are peach.
You can say 'I see peach!' or 'I want the peach one.'
nouns.colors.more.tan: Tan is a color you can see all around you.
Things like sand and a teddy bear are tan.
You can say 'I see tan!' or 'I want the tan one.'
nouns.shapes.circle: It is perfectly round with no corners.
You can find a circle in a ball or a wheel.
nouns.shapes.square: It has four equal sides and four corners.
You can find a square in a window or a cracker.
nouns.shapes.triangle: It has three sides and three corners.
You can find a triangle in a slice of pizza or a party hat.
nouns.shapes.rectangle: It has four sides — two long and two short.
You can find a rectangle in a door or a book.
nouns.shapes.oval: It is like a stretched-out circle, shaped like an egg.
You can find an oval in an egg.
nouns.shapes.diamond: It has four points and looks like a tilted square.
You can find a diamond in a kite.
nouns.shapes.heart: It has two bumps on top and a point at the bottom.
You can find a heart in a valentine.
nouns.shapes.star: You can find a star in a star up in the sky.
nouns.shapes.crescent: You can find a crescent in the moon at night.
nouns.shapes.hexagon: You can find a hexagon in a honeycomb.
nouns.shapes.octagon: You can find an octagon in a stop sign.
nouns.shapes.pentagon: You can find a pentagon in a drawing of a house.
nouns.shapes.arrow: It is a line with a point that shows which way to go.
You can find an arrow in a sign pointing the way.
nouns.shapes.cross: It has one line up-and-down and one line across.
You can find a cross in a plus sign.
